problem_addressed: Deep Reinforcement Learning for quadrotor flight control typically relies on Domain Randomization for sim-to-real transfer, resulting in overly conservative policies that struggle with dynamic disturbances.
core_claim: We propose a novel adaptive control architecture that actively perceives and reacts to instantaneous perturbations through a Residual Dynamics Predictor and data-efficient calibration bridge.
key_contributions: Training an optimal outer-loop policy and replacing its reliance on ground-truth disturbance data with a Residual Dynamics Predictor (RDP)
Introducing a data-efficient linear calibration bridge and online thrust correction mechanism for seamless hardware transfer
novelty: The Residual Dynamics Predictor (RDP) estimates external forces and moments online using only the history of states and control actions, eliminating the need for ground-truth disturbance data or conservative Domain Randomization policies.
method_summary: The approach trains an optimal outer-loop policy, replaces ground-truth disturbance data with a Residual Dynamics Predictor that estimates 6D perturbations from state/action history, and uses a linear calibration bridge with online thrust correction for hardware transfer.
method_components: Residual Dynamics Predictor (RDP) using GRU networks
Linear calibration bridge for sim-to-real transfer
key_innovation: The Residual Dynamics Predictor (RDP) enables adaptive control by estimating unobserved 6D environmental perturbations purely from temporal history of vehicle states and low-level actuation signals, allowing the controller to react to instantaneous disturbances without direct sensing.
assumptions: Symmetric cross quadrotor configuration with defined center-of-gravity offsets
Availability of state history and control action history for disturbance estimation
Ability to perform online calibration with minimal flight data
theoretical_grounding: The paper provides mathematical models for quadrotor dynamics including aerodynamic forces and moments, but does not include formal proofs or theoretical bounds for the proposed approach.
headline_result: Adaptive Controller with RDP achieves RMSE of 0.024 m (0% payload)
ablations: RDP provides latent disturbance representation that accounts for modeling mismatches
improvement_over_baseline: Adaptive Controller with RDP reduces RMSE by up to 0.136 m compared to Base Controller
best_result: Adaptive Controller with RDP achieves RMSE of 0.024 m (0% payload)
title: Adaptive Outer-Loop Control of Quadrotors via Reinforcement Learning
